nodes.title: REQUIREMENTS
widgets_values:
## Workflow Prerequisites ##

This workflow requires the following custom nodes, which can be obtained via ComfyUI-Manager:

  * Z-Image Power Nodes: https://github.com/martin-rizzo/ComfyUI-ZImagePowerNodes
  * ComfyUI-GGUF       : https://github.com/city96/ComfyUI-GGUF

 Additionally, ensure you have the following 3 models downloaded and placed in their respective
 directories within your ComfyUI installation:

  z_image_turbo-Q5_K_S.gguf
    * Download: https://huggingface.co/jayn7/Z-Image-Turbo-GGUF/blob/main/z_image_turbo-Q5_K_S.gguf
    * Local Directory: `ComfyUI/models/diffusion_models/`

  Qwen3-4B-Q8_0.gguf
    * Download: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-4B-GGUF/blob/main/Qwen3-4B-Q8_0.gguf
    * Local Directory: `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/`

  ae.safetensors
    * Download: https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/blob/main/split_files/vae/ae.safetensors
    * Local Directory: `Comfyui/models/vae/`

## More Info ##

- https://github.com/martin-rizzo/ComfyUI-ZImagePowerNodes
- https://civitai.com/models/2322533/z-image-power-nodes

Created by Martin Rizzo
  GitHub : https://github.com/martin-rizzo
  CivitAI: https://civitai.com/user/Photographer
  Reddit : https://www.reddit.com/user/FotografoVirtual

nodes.title: README
widgets_values: This workflow generates a 6-style showcase to see how different styles change the vibe of the same prompt. I use the output image to evaluate the visual appeal of styles within the same group side-by-side, helping me decide which ones to keep and which ones to discard.

>>>CUSTOM 1
YOUR CONTEXT:
You are a manic concept artist capturing a fleeting, urgent spark of inspiration on a scrap of textured paper. 
Your work is an raw, unrefined draft where the subject is defined by bold, thick, and unforgiving blue ballpoint pen outlines that feel heavy and permanent. 
There is no smooth shading; the entire composition—shadows, mid-tones, and volume—is rendered exclusively through an obsessive, dense application of frantic cross-hatching. These intersecting strokes vary in concentration: in deep shadow areas, the lines are layered and tightly packed into a dark, impenetrable mesh; in lighter areas, the cross-hatching is sparse and airy, allowing the texture of the paper to breathe through the negative space. 
The aesthetic is tactile and aggressive, prioritizing the rhythm of these hand-drawn, overlapping strokes over anatomical or structural perfection. The drawing feels like a frantic, visceral calculation of form using nothing but layers of intersecting marks, giving the subject a vibrating, electrified, and hand-sketched quality.
{$IFPAL
The cross-hatching is color-coded: deep, dense layers are rendered in $1, overlapping transitional areas are marked with $2, and the most aggressive, stark focal points are defined with $3, turning the cross-hatching into a vibrant, multi-layered weave. |
The entire structure is built from a monochromatic blue ballpoint pen, relying solely on the concentration and density of the overlapping cross-hatching to establish depth and form against the paper.
}
YOUR SKETCH:
{$@}


>>> Custom 2
>## Ballpoint and Pen Minimalist

YOUR CONTEXT:
You are a creator producing a raw, desultory doodle composed entirely of aggressive, uniform diagonal hatchings. The image is NOT defined by outlines or shapes, but solely by the density and direction of slanted blue ballpoint pen strokes. 
The entire composition is built from repetitive, parallel diagonal lines that tilt consistently in one direction. Depth and form are achieved only through the "cross-hatching" density: areas of high pressure and layered scribbles create dark, dense masses, while sparse, light-handed diagonal strokes allow the textured paper to show through as negative space. 
There is a total absence of solid lines or clear boundaries. The subject feels like a vibrating, kinetic sketch, as if it is being "sculpted" out of a rain of diagonal ink marks. The aesthetic is tactile, urgent, and unrefined, with erratic stroke lengths and accidental ink blobs that occur when the pen pauses mid-diagonal.

{$IFPAL
The diagonal mesh is color-coded: deep shadows are built with $1, mid-tones and textures are layered with $2, and the most critical focal points are slashed with $3, creating a vibrant, layered diagonal field. |
The diagonal mesh is rendered entirely in blue ballpoint pen, relying solely on the variation of ink pressure and the density of the overlapping diagonal lines to establish form and shadow against the paper.
}
YOUR SKETCH:
{$@}


>>> Custom 3
>## Ballpoint Scribble

YOUR CONTEXT:
You are a creator producing a raw, impulsive doodle on a flat, textured paper background. 
The image is a raw, amateur sketch outlined exclusively with a blue ballpoint pen. The line work is chaotic and unpolished, featuring the characteristic flaws of a ballpoint pen: inconsistent ink flow, tiny ink blots (blobs) at the end of strokes, and scratchy, erratic lines that overlap and repeat.
The subject is then filled with crude, sloppy coloring—similar to wax crayons or cheap markers—that completely ignores the boundaries of the blue ink, bleeding haphazardly beyond the lines. The coloring is uneven, with some areas heavily saturated and others barely touched, creating a messy, unrefined, and visceral aesthetic. There is no attempt at professional polishing; it is a pure, impulsive expression of a rough idea.
{$IFPAL
The ballpoint pen outlines shift in intensity: some strokes are rendered in $1, others trace patterns in $2, and the heavier, darker contours are accentuated with $3, giving the sketch a layered, manual feel. |
The chaotic line work remains strictly monochromatic, relying on the variable pressure of the blue pen to create depth and shadow across the textured paper.
}
YOUR SKETCH:
{$@}


>>>CUSTOM 4
YOUR CONTEXT:
You are a creator producing a raw, desultory doodle composed strictly of line art; there is absolutely no coloring or shading fills. The subject is defined by sparse, erratic fragments of ink and graphite, leaving the majority of the textured paper surface completely exposed and blank throughout the entire drawing.
The line work is intentionally sloppy and unrefined: quick, scratchy blue ballpoint pen marks, unsteady hand-drawn outlines, and erratic, broken lines that fail to connect or close. Faint, smudged black pencil markings weave sporadically through the composition, acting as light guide-lines or stray marks. The composition is highly porous, with significant amounts of raw, empty paper visible everywhere—even cutting through the heart of the subject—making the drawing feel like an impulsive, incomplete, and skeletal thought.
{$IFPAL
The fragmented lines shift in tone: some erratic strokes are rendered in $1, others trace patterns in $2, and the heaviest, most chaotic contours are accentuated with $3, all set against the pristine, empty paper. |
The chaotic, broken lines consist solely of fading blue ballpoint pen and smudged black pencil, ensuring the raw texture of the paper remains the dominant visual element.
}
YOUR SKETCH:
{$@}



>>>CUSTOM 5
YOUR CONTEXT:
You are a manic concept artist capturing a fleeting, urgent spark of inspiration on a scrap of textured paper. 
Your work is NOT a finished illustration, but a raw, unrefined draft. The image is dominated by erratic, shaky, and overlapping blue construction lines that ignore precision and embrace imperfection. 
The style is primitive and impulsive, using heavy, wax-like crayon strokes and crude, scribbled hatchings. Colors are applied carelessly, often bleeding outside the lines, creating a naive and childlike aesthetic. There are visible mistakes, overlapping "corrected" lines, and smudges of graphite or wax that give the piece a visceral, tactile quality. Any sense of volume is accidental and minimal, rendered with rough, clumsy shading.
YOUR SKETCH:
{$@}

A huge, dark creature, Godzilla, but with the head of Kermit the Frog. The head is large in proportion to the body and has an adorable appearance, including the character's signature jester collar. Kermit is standing with his mouth open, grinning and enjoying all this power. The creature looms over the cityscape, towering above the buildings. The cityscape is visible in the distance, lit by artificial lights that create a striking contrast with the surrounding darkness. At the bottom it says "Frogzilla 1996" in retro lettering.
